name: Nossa Senhora da Graça Loop
name_original: Giro ad anello da Sobreira de Mondim verso Bilhó
description: Tackle the difficult Nossa Senhora da Graça Loop, a 32.5-mile mountain bike trail with 4428 feet of gain and waterfall views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Atei features diverse terrain, including natural parks, mountainous areas, and varied landscapes. The region is characterized by significant elevation changes, with routes traversing through areas that may include ancient lava flows, maize fields, and alpine ridges. These physical features provide a range of challenges and scenic backdrops for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Atei The most popular mountain bike trail is Nossa Senhora da Graça Loop, a 32.5 miles (52.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Loop tour from Sobreira de Mondim in the Alvão Natural Park, a difficult 46.2 miles (74.3 km) path. This route explores the Alvão Natural Park, offering extensive views and challenging climbs. Local mountain bikers also love the Monte Farinha and Senhora da Graça Loop, a 12.6 miles (20.2 km) trail leading through hilly terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 55 minutes. Mountain biking around Atei is defined by natural parks, significant elevation changes, and diverse landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult, long-distance routes. The routes in Atei are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 250 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Atei's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Atei?
A: There are 18 mountain bike trails around Atei, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The region is known for its diverse terrain, including natural parks and mountainous areas.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails for beginners in Atei?
A: While Atei primarily features moderate to difficult mountain bike routes, there are no trails specifically classified as easy. However, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2321383987/monte-farinha-and-senhora-da-graca-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Farinha and Senhora da Graça Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option, covering 20.2 km in about 1 hour 55 minutes, which might be suitable for riders with some experience looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: What are the most challenging mountain bike routes in Atei?
A: Atei offers several challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2321440786/loop-tour-from-sobreira-de-mondim-in-the-alvao-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop tour from Sobreira de Mondim in the Alvão Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 74.3 km path with significant elevation gain, typically taking over 7 hours. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2321408594/nossa-senhora-da-graca-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nossa Senhora da Graça Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 52.2 km difficult trail that takes around 5 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike options in Atei?
A: Given that all trails in Atei are classified as moderate or difficult, there are no routes specifically recommended as family-friendly for young children or beginners. Riders should assess their family's skill and endurance levels carefully before attempting any of the routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Atei?
A: Mountain biking around Atei features diverse terrain, including natural parks, mountainous areas, and varied landscapes. You can expect significant elevation changes, with routes traversing through areas that may include ancient lava flows, maize fields, and alpine ridges. These physical features provide a range of challenges and scenic backdrops.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Atei?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Atei are circular. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2321408594/nossa-senhora-da-graca-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nossa Senhora da Graça Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2321440786/loop-tour-from-sobreira-de-mondim-in-the-alvao-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop tour from Sobreira de Mondim in the Alvão Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2321383987/monte-farinha-and-senhora-da-graca-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Farinha and Senhora da Graça Loop&lt;/a&gt;, all designed as loops for convenient riding.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Atei?
A: The mountain bike routes in Atei are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 250 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Atei's varied terrain, often praising the challenging climbs and extensive views, especially within the Alvão Natural Park.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions near the mountain bike trails in Atei?
A: While exploring the trails, you might encounter several interesting points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/729193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fisgas de Ermelo Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; offer a stunning natural sight. You could also pass through &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/328221" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermelo Village&lt;/a&gt; or see the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4518272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arco de Baúlhe Railway Station&lt;/a&gt;. For those interested in longer routes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3568170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tâmega Line Ecotrail&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through the Alvão Natural Park?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2321440786/loop-tour-from-sobreira-de-mondim-in-the-alvao-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop tour from Sobreira de Mondim in the Alvão Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent route that explores this natural park, offering extensive views and challenging climbs through its protected landscapes.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail available in Atei?
A: The longest mountain bike trail in Atei is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2321440786/loop-tour-from-sobreira-de-mondim-in-the-alvao-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop tour from Sobreira de Mondim in the Alvão Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 74.3 km and is classified as difficult. It typically takes over 7 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that include historical sites like castles?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23741385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelo de Arnoia – Penedo das Grades loop from Celorico de Basto&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 67.1 km route that incorporates the historic Castelo de Arnoia, offering a blend of challenging riding and cultural exploration.
tips: From this hairpin bend you have a spectacular view. The spontaneous vegetation extends into a carpet of dry grass and low shrubs, typical of the hilly environment. A few scattered trees, with foliage of a more intense green, dot the landscape, adding depth and contrast.

The waterfall cascades down a steep rock face, formed by large boulders and outcrops of dark and gray stone. The water, a brilliant white color, splits into multiple streams as it descends, creating a multi-layered effect. 
Around the waterfall and along the rocky slopes, vegetation is sparse but tenacious.
The trail follows the Cabril River and passes by the famous swings of Vilar de Viando. The stone bottom is really fun!

faqs.question: How difficult is the Nossa Senhora da Graça Loop for mountain biking?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike loop is rated as difficult. It covers a distance of approximately 52.2 km with a significant elevation gain of about 1,350 meters, making it suitable for experienced riders with good fitness levels.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, typical of mountain biking in the region. You can expect a mix of trails, including sections along the Cabril River, and potentially some challenging climbs and descents given the overall difficulty and elevation profile.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or natural features along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several points of interest. You'll encounter a Panoramic view from the hairpin, providing stunning vistas. The trail also passes by the impressive Fisgas de Ermelo Waterfalls, a significant natural landmark.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Nossa Senhora da Graça Loop trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for this exact loop are not provided, trails in this region typically have designated parking areas near common starting points in villages like Sobreira de Mondim or Bilhó. It's advisable to check local maps or community information for the most convenient trailhead parking.

faqs.question: Is the Nossa Senhora da Graça Loop suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Given its difficult rating, significant distance (52.2 km), and considerable elevation gain (1,350 m), this specific loop is generally not recommended for beginners or young families. More experienced mountain bikers will find it a rewarding challenge.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Nossa Senhora da Graça Loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this loop is typically during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and the weather is generally more stable. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder, wetter conditions, making some sections more challenging.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You will ride along parts of the [CIMA PR10] Nossa Senhora da Graça (monte Farinha), Caminhos da Senhora da Graça: Ermelo - Senhora da Graça, Caminhos da Senhora da Graça - Bilhó - Senhora da Graça, and the Fisgas de Ermelo trail, among others.

faqs.question: Is the Nossa Senhora da Graça Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this loop is located within the Parque Natural do Alvão, dogs are generally permitted but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Parque Natural do Alvão?
faqs.answer: There are typically no entrance fees or specific permits required for general access to the Parque Natural do Alvão for activities like mountain biking. However, always respect park regulations, especially concerning wildlife, waste, and fire prevention.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends near villages like Sobreira de Mondim and Bilhó. These smaller communities may offer limited local cafes or guesthouses. For a wider range of services, you might need to venture to slightly larger towns in the vicinity of the Parque Natural do Alvão.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this difficult loop?
faqs.answer: For a difficult, 52 km mountain bike ride, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit (including spare tubes/patches), a first-aid kit, appropriate cycling gear, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. A GPS device or a downloaded map of the route is also highly recommended.
